dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 4 for unknown problem/person down at --Address Redacted--.
Hi, I'm Taylor and this is Jordan. What happened that made you call 911 today?
We found a man sitting in the road with his pants down. He won't respond.
He doesn't open his eyes to voice. I saw no verbal response. Motor response seems to be localizing.
Yes, he moved when touched, but kept his eyes closed the whole time.
Airway is patent with no signs of obstruction or abnormal secretions.
Respiratory rate is 32 breaths per minute, rapid and regular. No cyanosis noted.
SpO2 is 97 on room air. Skin is warm, dry, and normal in color.
Pulse is 160, regular in rate.
Breathing is at 32 per minute, fast with no signs of distress.
Blood pressure is 125 over 67. Looks reasonably stable for now.
Glucose is at 0. We need to recheck later for context.
SpO2 is 97. No signs of hypoxemia at this point.
I'm going to apply 4-lead ECG now for cardiac monitoring.
4-lead ECG shows sinus tach, no acute MI signs observed. We'll now do a 12-lead.
No rashes or trauma observed except minor abrasion on right middle finger. Skin is normal, warm, and dry.
Pupils are 5mm, equal and reactive to light. Facial features are symmetrical with no droop noted.
The patient did not give any ID or personal information. No ID found on his person.
He was sitting in the road when officers approached. He resisted movement, was placed on the ground, but no injury done.
When touched, he flinched but otherwise did not show signs of pain. No chest pain reported.
We couldn't get allergy history or current medications due to patient's silence.
Activating protocol 3-15 for altered mental status with a GCS of 7.
Field impression is altered mental status and possible behavioral emergency. No neurological deficit on examination.
Let me check the pulse again-now at 128 with 28 respirations. Blood pressure is 127 over 64.
SpO2 is 96. Glucose is now 138. Stays stable overall.
12-lead EKG confirms sinus tach. Minor evidence of LVH noted. There were signs consistent with possible atrial flutter during monitoring.
Still no identifying information. Medical history and medications are unknown.
Bystander confirms patient resisted gently but was not injured due to his restraint. Was placed in handcuffs for transport.
No pain reported. No signs of acute respiratory distress.
Final vitals: pulse 119, respirations 28, BP 138/82. Glucose at 138 and SpO2 remains 96.
We're transporting to [COMPANY_NAME] for full evaluation. Tracking for possible atrial flutter or LVH findings.
Patient secured to the stretcher. Due to altered mental status, restraints were not used.
No acute clinical deterioration in transport. Patient remains uncommunicative and altered.
Please note the abrasion and old forehead injury found during exam. Monitor EKG for possible changes.
